description_long: This is a tall, cylindrical stone obelisk with a weathered, beige surface and visible inscriptions carved into its upper section. The inscriptions appear to be in a classical script, possibly Latin, and are arranged in horizontal lines that wrap partially around the shaft. The obelisk's surface is rough and uneven, with signs of erosion and age, giving it an ancient and historical appearance. The base is simple and flat, resting on a square platform that appears to be made of a different, darker material. The tip of the obelisk is rounded and slightly irregular, lacking any pointed or decorative finial. There are no visible decorative motifs or surrounding features, emphasizing the monument's austere and utilitarian design.
description: A tall, cylindrical stone obelisk with weathered inscriptions and a rough, beige surface, standing on a simple square base. The tip is rounded and irregular, with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the most detailed and legible section of the carved inscriptions on the upper shaft.
description_view_1: Side view with inscriptions still visible but less prominent, showing more of the cylindrical curvature.
description_view_2: Rear view with a mostly plain, unadorned surface and minimal visible inscriptions.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with faint traces of inscriptions and a generally smooth, undecorated surface.
attribute_inscription: Carved inscriptions are present on the upper section of the obelisk, featuring horizontal lines of classical script, likely Latin. The text is weathered but partially legible, with the most detail visible in the front view.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple, flat square platform, slightly darker than the obelisk itself, with no decorative features or steps.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is rounded and irregular, lacking any pointed or decorative elements.
